scenario.prompt: Your class is making a project about a country. You volunteer to...

options.text: Write the narration and descriptions
Design the maps and visual layout
Compose a short song or chant about the country
Build a 3D model of a famous landmark

scenario.prompt: Your group has a $20 budget for a class party. You...

options.text: Make a spreadsheet to compare prices and maximize what you can buy
Ask everyone what they most want and find a compromise
Quietly think through what would make the party most meaningful
Suggest spending it on a nature activity like a garden visit

scenario.prompt: Your school needs a new mural for the entrance hall. You offer to...

options.text: Sketch the layout and lead the visual design
Design it around a theme about life or community
Build an interactive digital version for the school website
Write a poem or quote that captures the school's spirit

scenario.prompt: Your class is putting together an assembly performance. You most want to...

options.text: Compose or arrange the music for it
Plan the timing, transitions, and running order
Choreograph the movement and dance sections
Coordinate the team and make sure everyone feels included

scenario.prompt: After a stressful exam week, you recover best by...

options.text: Going for a run, shooting hoops, or doing something physical
Spending time alone — journaling or just thinking quietly
Going outside for a walk — nature completely resets you
Sitting with bigger questions — what's the point of all this pressure?

scenario.prompt: A classmate seems left out of the group chat. You...

options.text: Reach out in person and make sure they feel included
Add them to the group or set up a new chat that includes everyone
Write them a friendly note or message
Figure out why the group dynamic shifted and suggest a fix

scenario.prompt: You're choosing what kind of project to do for a free assignment. You want something that...

options.text: Lets you explore your own thoughts, values, and identity deeply
Lets you design or illustrate something visually striking
Lets you write or perform original music
Involves movement, building, or hands-on making

scenario.prompt: On a school field trip to a forest, you spend most of your time...

options.text: Examining plants, insects, and tracking what you observe
Chatting and exploring with your friends
Thinking about what the forest symbolizes and its deeper meaning
Photographing everything and thinking about how to edit and share it

scenario.prompt: Your class debates whether AI will ever be truly 'conscious.' You...

options.text: Jump in — consciousness and what makes us human fascinate you
Write a structured argument outlining both sides
Map out the logical steps of what consciousness would actually require
Draw a diagram of the relationship between brain, AI, and awareness

scenario.prompt: You want to document your class trip. You...

options.text: Build a quick website or edit a video to capture and share it
Write a song or poem about the day
Keep a private journal of your own reflections and feelings
Focus on nature photography — plants, animals, and landscapes

likert.statement: I enjoy reading books or stories in my free time.
I find it easy to explain my ideas clearly to others.
I like writing — journals, stories, or creative pieces.
I notice when someone uses an interesting or unusual word.
I enjoy solving math or logic problems, even outside of school.
I like finding patterns and figuring out how things connect.
I prefer having a clear step-by-step approach when solving problems.
I enjoy strategy games like chess, Sudoku, or coding puzzles.
I can easily picture how an object looks from a different angle.
I enjoy drawing, doodling, or designing things visually.
I have a good sense of direction and rarely get confused about where I am.
When working through a problem, I often sketch or diagram it.
I can remember a melody after hearing it just once or twice.
Music strongly affects my mood — the right song changes how I feel.
I often catch myself humming, tapping, or keeping a beat without realizing it.
I immediately notice when something sounds off-key or a rhythm is wrong.
I learn better by doing than by reading or listening.
I enjoy sports, dancing, building things, or other hands-on activities.
I find it hard to sit still for long stretches of time.
I pick up physical activities quickly and have good coordination.
I enjoy working in groups more than working alone.
I can usually sense how someone is feeling even before they say anything.
Friends often come to me when they need advice or someone to talk to.
I'm good at getting a group to cooperate and stay on track.
I think deeply about who I am, what I value, and what I want in life.
I prefer to work alone and set my own goals and pace.
I have a clear sense of my own strengths and weaknesses.
I track my feelings and reflect on my emotions — through journaling or quiet thinking.
I notice details about nature — plants, animals, weather — that others often miss.
I feel more relaxed and happy when I spend time outdoors.
I enjoy learning the names and facts about different animals or plants.
I care deeply about environmental issues and protecting the natural world.
I often wonder about big questions — like why we exist or what happens after we die.
Deep questions about fairness, meaning, or purpose genuinely fascinate me.
I enjoy conversations about consciousness, the universe, or what makes us human.
I think a lot about what it means to live a good and meaningful life.
I pick up new apps, devices, or software quickly without much help.
I enjoy creating things digitally — videos, games, websites, or graphics.
I naturally understand how digital tools and systems work.
I feel comfortable finding information online and judging whether it's trustworthy.
